headline: President Trump Posts On Truth Social "Khamenei, one of the most evil people in History, is dead. This is not only Justice for the people of Iran, but for all Great Americans..." ".. The heavy and pinpoint bombing, however, will continue, uninterrupted throughout the week or, as long as necessary to achieve our objective of PEACE THROUGHOUT THE MIDDLE EAST AND, INDEED, THE WORLD!..."
Khamenei Killed In Israeli Strike on Compound
summary: On Saturday, Israeli officials said Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed after an Israeli airstrike hit his compound in Tehran, a development that could accelerate a power struggle inside the Islamic Republic and reshape Tehran's regional posture.

headline: CIA Warns Iran's IRGC Might Maintain Power Despite Khamenei's Removal
summary: On Saturday, the U.S. Central Intelligence Agency weighed scenarios in which the U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran could still leave the country led by hardline Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps figures even if Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei were removed, according to people briefed on the intelligence.

headline: Micron's $2.75 Billion India Facility Targets AI-Driven Chip Boom
summary: Micron opened its $2.75 billion Gujarat assembly-and-test plant to ramp chip output for AI-driven memory demand, while traders weighed mixed technical signals ahead of its March 18 update.

headline: Thomas Edison Kept 'To-Do Lists' All His Life, Which Sometimes Ran Pages: Here Is Why They Have The Potential To Transform Your Life Even Today
summary: In 1888, Thomas Edison's punch list mixed urgent tasks with ambitious goals. This highly structured approach to invention resulted in 1,093 US patents.

headline: Second HeLa Lawsuit Resolved As Novartis Reaches Confidential Settlement
summary: Novartis reached a confidential settlement with Henrietta Lacks' family, resolving a second lawsuit over the nonconsensual use of HeLa cells.

headline: Russia-Ukraine Local Ceasefire Enables Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Power Line Repairs, IAEA Confirms
summary:
Russia and Ukraine agreed to a local ceasefire to allow repairs to backup power lines at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant, the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) said on Friday.



Rafael Mariano Grossi, IAEA Director General, said "demining activities are ongoing to ensure safe access for the repair teams."

headline: OpenAI Lands Pentagon Deal Hours After Trump Blacklists Anthropic— Altman Says Department Of War Agreed To AI Safety Guardrails
summary: OpenAI struck a deal on Friday to deploy its AI tools inside the Pentagon's classified systems, hours after the Trump administration formally blacklisted rival Anthropic.

headline: Blackstone CEO Schwarzman Earns Over $1.2 Billion In 2025 Amid Record Profits
summary: Blackstone CEO Steve Schwarzman earned $1.24 billion in total compensation in 2025, slightly below his 2022 record, while the firm reported record earnings for the year.

headline: Trump Bans Anthropic As Pentagon Reportedly Accepts OpenAI's Military AI Safeguards— Anthony Scaramucci, Ilya Sutskever, Ross Gerber Weigh In
summary: President Donald Trump ordered federal agencies to phase out Anthropic after the Pentagon reportedly agreed to safety safeguards proposed by OpenAI for classified military AI use, triggering sharp political and industry reactions.

headline: Meta, YouTube, TikTok And Others Bag A Win As Federal Court Stops Virginia's Under-16 Social Media Restrictions Backed By 30 States
summary: A federal judge in Virginia temporarily blocked the state's one-hour daily social media limit for minors under 16, siding with tech companies including Meta and YouTube in a lawsuit brought by NetChoice, ruling the law likely violates First Amendment protections.

headline: Elon Musk's xAI Tools Under Fire From US Government Over Safety And Reliability Concerns
summary: Elon Musk's xAI faces federal scrutiny over the safety and reliability of its AI tools as the Pentagon selects its Grok chatbot for classified use.

headline: Trump Escalates Pressure On Cuba With Fuel Quarantine: 'Maybe We'll Have A Friendly Takeover'
summary: Donald Trump escalated pressure on Cuba by imposing a fuel blockade and halting Venezuelan oil support, suggesting a possible "friendly takeover" as the island faces looming blackouts, humanitarian strain and rising diplomatic tensions following a deadly coast guard incident.
